markdown:
<title level="1" right="Background " pfs="Standard">[Press-Ganged (G&G)](/Backgrounds.aspx?ID=635)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 124

 

</column>

Was it five shots or six? It doesn’t really matter in the end, because after those nice folks bought all your drinks, you woke up on the lowest rung of the ladder. Whether a crewmate on a ship, a conscript in an army, or something worse, you’ve nowhere to go but up. This background generally requires buy-in with the entire group to tell a story allowing you to play a character starting their adventuring due to circumstances outside their own control. However, you can also play a character who was once press-ganged and has since escaped that life. If you do, press-ganged doesn’t have the uncommon trait, and it has the same mechanical effects either way.

Choose two attribute boosts. One must be to Strength
or Constitution, and one is a free attribute boost.

You're trained in the [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) skill, and the [Labor Lore](/skills/lore) skill. You gain the [Armor Assist](/Feats.aspx?ID=2109) skill feat.
name: Press-Ganged (G&G)

**Attributes**
Strength, Constitution

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3), Labor [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8)

**Feat**
[Armor Assist](/Feats.aspx?ID=2109)
</additional-info>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Background " pfs="Standard">[Sheriff](/Backgrounds.aspx?ID=636)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 124

 

</column>

You hold an official title of sheriff or deputy in a small community. Those that would do harm to others flee your gaze, for they know it brings the promise of castigation. You protect your town, watch over those who have placed their trust in you, and make sure those that would do others harm are brought to justice.

Choose two attribute boosts. One must be to Dexterity
or Charisma, and one is a free attribute boost.

You're trained in the [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) skill, and the [Hunting Lore](/skills/lore) skill. You gain the [Group Coercion](/Feats.aspx?ID=788) skill feat.
name: Sheriff

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Background " pfs="Standard">[Otherworldly Mission](/Backgrounds.aspx?ID=637)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 125

 

</column>

You’re called to serve a specific purpose—your deity told you so, and so it must be true. Maybe you grew up knowing all along, or maybe it came to you one day in a vision, clear as a bell and twice as loud. You have some task that only you can accomplish. You mission might be as dangerous as killing a deadly tyrant or as simple as opening a particular door on a particular day.

You should work with your GM on how to handle the truth of your character’s backstory or leave it to the GM to decide in secret. Is this call to action a message from the character’s own mind, are they being manipulated by some manner of supernatural entity, or are they actually getting marching orders from one of the deities of the setting? 

Choose two ability boosts. One must be to **Wisdom** or **Charisma**, and one is a free ability boost.

You’re trained in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46). Once per adventure, you can ask the voice you believe to be a deity for orders and get some kind of instruction—you never get any kind of explanation, simply a command to go somewhere or do something. Following those commands isn’t always safe, but it’s usually interesting.
name: Otherworldly Mission

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Background " pfs="Standard">[Revenant](/Backgrounds.aspx?ID=638)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 125

 

</column>

You died. No real doubt about that. Bullet to the brain or knife to the throat, you were dead as dead can be. Then you got back up again. Maybe you had some unfinished business, or maybe you were just so tough and so mean that Hell itself spat you out. Either way, you came back for a reason. 

Choose two ability boosts. One must be to **Constitution** or **Charisma**, and one is a free ability boost.

You’re trained in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) and [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=39) Lore. You’re still alive, not undead, but you have the negative healing ability, which means you’re harmed by positive damage and healed by negative effects as if you were undead.
name: Revenant

**Skills**

</additional-info>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Background " pfs="Standard">[Tall Tale](/Backgrounds.aspx?ID=639)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 125

 

</column>

You’ve ridden a tornado, lassoed a river, mooned a dragon under full moon, and shot six zombies with one bullet. That’s what the stories say, anyway, and even if people don’t strictly believe them, they are curious about you. Certainly, the stories keep spreading, and it would appear that you either have a unique destiny or are trying to convince the world that you do.

Choose two ability boosts. One must be to **Constitution** or **Charisma**, and one is a free ability boost.

You’re trained in [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45). You gain the [Impressive Performance](/Feats.aspx?ID=5159) skill feat. You can gain the Connections skill feat later without being expert in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) or having [Courtly Graces](/Feats.aspx?ID=5138)—this represents less traditional social connections and more the way that your legend has spread, and you’ve learned to leverage your legend, with people wanting to meet you and see you for themselves.
name: Tall Tale

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Background " pfs="Limited">[Deckhand](/Backgrounds.aspx?ID=640)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Beginner Box: Hero's Handbook](/Sources.aspx?ID=325) pg. 18

 

</column>

The rolling waves of the high seas, the constant sway of the deck underfoot, and the creaking heights of a ship's rigging are as familiar to you as solid ground. You might have worked on a simple fishing boat, a wealthy merchant's galley, a mighty warship—or even a pirate's caravel. Whether your crew retired, your ship sank, or you've turned to adventuring so you can keep all the treasure for yourself, you still retain an excellent sense of balance and quick reflexes.

Choose two attribute boosts. One must be to Strength
or Dexterity, and one is a free attribute boost.

You're trained in the [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) skill, and the [Sailing Lore](/skills/lore) skill. You gain the [Cat Fall](/Feats.aspx?ID=765) skill feat.
name: Deckhand

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Background " pfs="Limited">[Anti-Thrune Saboteur](/Backgrounds.aspx?ID=641)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hell's Destiny Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=350) pg. 8

 

</column>

House Thrune has cast its tyrannical shadow over Cheliax for over 80 years, and you long to topple the dynasty and free southwest Avistan from its grip. You might be a spy for Andoran or another active combatant in the ongoing war, or perhaps you’re a rebellious soul who grew up under Thrune’s thumb and dreams of a better future. Whatever the case, you’re a versed saboteur ready to destroy Cheliax’s armies from behind enemy lines. 

Choose two ability boosts. One must be to **Dexterity** or **Charisma**, and one is a free attribute boost. 

You’re trained in either [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) or [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50), as well as either the [Cheliax Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) or Engineering Lore skill. If you chose Deception, you gain the [Lengthy Diversion](/Feats.aspx?ID=5176) skill feat. If you chose Thievery, you gain the [Dirty Trick](/Feats.aspx?ID=6472) skill feat.
name: Anti-Thrune Saboteur

<title level="1" right="Background " pfs="Limited">[Archdevil Apostate](/Backgrounds.aspx?ID=642)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hell's Destiny Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=350) pg. 8, [Hellbreakers Players Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=317) pg. 7

 

</column>

You once worshipped a powerful [archdevil](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=4) but have turned away from fiendish practices. Whether you walked away due to moral compunctions or cold pragmatism, you know your soul was once bound for [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42) and you can only pray that your new path erases the sins you’ve committed.

Choose two attribute boosts. One must be to Charisma
or Intelligence, and one is a free attribute boost.

You're trained in the [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) skill, and the [Devil Lore](/skills/lore) skill. You gain the [Student of the Canon](/Feats.aspx?ID=5219) skill feat.
name: Archdevil Apostate

<title level="1" right="Background " pfs="Limited">[Defiant Noble](/Backgrounds.aspx?ID=643)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hell's Destiny Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=350) pg. 8

 

</column>

Any Chelaxian aristocrat is trained to acknowledge House Thrune’s power and sing its praises, lest their entire dynasty be devastated and absorbed by a stronger house. You grew up among the Cheliax’s upper crust and learned to play its game, all the while harboring secret dreams of reform— if not outright rebellion. The ongoing war would be an opportunity to win glory and accolades on the battlefield in Thrune’s name. But while others are distracted, you’d rather undermine your rivals, aid the secessionist ambitions of Cheliax’s vassals, or even overthrow House Thrune itself! At least outwardly, your family will denounce your action, even if they might silently pray for your success. 

Choose two ability boosts. One must be to **Strength** or **Charisma**, and one is a free attribute boost. 

You’re trained in the [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) skill and either the [Cheliax Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) or Legal Lore skill. You gain the [Courtly Graces](/Feats.aspx?ID=5138) skill feat.
name: Defiant Noble

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Background " pfs="Limited">[Embargo Survivor](/Backgrounds.aspx?ID=644)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hell's Destiny Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=350) pg. 8

 

</column>

Soon after the outbreak of war, Cheliax sealed one of its greatest chokepoints: the Arch of Aroden, which spans the lone western entrance to the Inner Sea. A Chelaxian armada guards the strait and has prohibited nearly all traffic into or out of the nearby port of Corentyn. You might be a longtime Corentyn resident versed in shipping or a traveler whose ship became trapped by the sudden embargo, trapping you in the city. Whatever the case, you’ve had to learn quickly how to survive under martial law in an increasingly claustrophobic city with dwindling supplies and desperate civilians looking to you for leadership.

 Choose two ability boosts. One must be to **Constitution** or **Wisdom**, and one is a free attribute boost. 

You’re trained in [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) and either the [Sailing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) or Warfare Lore skill. You gain the Scrappy Defiance action 

**Scrappy Defiance** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** Your turn begins, and you are commanding a troop; **Effect** You encourage your followers to persevere against all hardships. The troop you command gains [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) equal to twice your level which last for 1 minutes. If your troop’s size would be reduced due to damage, you can attempt a DC 16 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333); on a success, your troop’s size isn’t reduced at that threshold until the end of your next turn.
name: Embargo Survivor

**Attributes**

**Skills**

</additional-info>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Background " pfs="Limited">[Hell-Hunted](/Backgrounds.aspx?ID=645)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hell's Destiny Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=350) pg. 9

 

</column>

Cheliax’s vengeance and Hellknights’ reach aren’t limited to southern Avistan. Whether you committed a grievous crime or have been targeted for disproportionate justice for a minor wrong, you’re in a tyrant’s crosshairs and have been on the run from the law. You might not have made it beyond Corentyn before realizing you’d never make it past the naval blockade alone. Perhaps you have trekked across continents while being hounded by Hellknights, and you’ve determined that the only way to end this twisted game is to confront Cheliax and the Hellknights on their own turf. Whatever the case, your ordeal has left you toughened and wise to infernal tactics. 

Choose two ability boosts. One must be to **Dexterity** or **Constitution**, and one is a free attribute boost. 

You’re trained in [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) and either the [Devil Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) or Hellknight Lore skill. You gain the [Terrain Stalker](/Feats.aspx?ID=5224) skill feat.
name: Hell-Hunted

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Background " pfs="Limited">[Scholar of Battlefields](/Backgrounds.aspx?ID=646)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hell's Destiny Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=350) pg. 9

 

</column>

Though you probably don’t revel in the violence of war, you are fascinated by battlefield tactics and regional conflicts’ effects on history. Perhaps you traveled to Cheliax to study its strategies, document its logistical innovations, and chronicle events. You might instead be an enterprising mercenary looking to sell your services in the conflict. No matter your original intentions, you’ve become mired in Corentyn’s martial law, and the more that Cheliax harms its own citizens to maintain wartime compliance, the more eager you’ve become to aid its enemies.

Choose two attribute boosts. One must be to Strength
or Intelligence, and one is a free attribute boost.

You're trained in the [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) skill, and the [Warfare Lore](/skills/lore) skill. You gain the Relentless Assault action. 

**Relentless Assault** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** A creature would succeed or critically succeed at a saving throw against the offensive ability of a troop you command; **Effect** With an encouraging command, you direct your troop to make a few additional attacks. The triggering creature rerolls its saving throw and uses the worse result
name: Scholar of Battlefields
